focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 11

After dinner the next day, by the second reading, the pattern was too clean to dismiss.

The base layer at the bottom of the Mark had strengthened again. Hermione wrote the new measurement in the ledger, checked last week's entry, then the week before. The figures did not wobble. They climbed. The lowest stratum, the one that did not belong to Voldemort's original binding and had no business thickening under any known treatment, was answering sustained contact by growing denser, more articulate, harder to separate from the rest of the curse structure.

Every long examination moved it.

That correlation stayed out of the ledger.

She copied down the safe numbers instead: lattice degradation, surface reactivity, residual pain response. No mention of what the buried layer did when her hand stayed on him too long. No mention of the way the whole configuration now altered fastest under her touch. If she wrote that part cleanly, she would have to decide whether the examinations were helping or teaching the Mark a new trick. She was not ready to make the call in ink.

Across the table Draco sat with his sleeve rolled past the elbow and a book open beside his right hand. He had stopped pretending to read three minutes earlier. She could tell by the stillness. Real reading changed his face in small ways. His mouth loosened. His eyes moved at a pace that had nothing to do with caution. Now he was holding himself still and letting her work, which was not the same thing.

"I'm going to push lower," she said.

His gaze dropped to the crystals arranged beside her wand, then to the ledger, then back to her face. "Lower than last time."

"I need the interface under the defensive layer. The readings keep blurring before I get there."

"And if it goes badly?"

"Then I stop....
open_hooks_to_carry: Post-drawing-room gentleness rendered in small domestic moments: shared meals, books left on chairs, tea made without asking
Draco cataloguing Hermione's expressions — her laugh, her sleeping in the library — registering that he is doing this
Theo Nott's visit: Draco with a friend, drier and funnier, the dynamic revealing who he is when not armored
prev_tail_excerpt:
She nodded once, not to him exactly but to the fact.

"I can't stay in here," she said.

"You shouldn't."

"And I can't promise tomorrow looks normal."

"I wasn't going to ask."

"Good."

She turned toward the door. Behind her she heard the scrape of chair legs against marble.

"Granger."

She stopped but did not turn.

His voice, when it came, was stripped so clean it was nearly toneless. "I am sorry that when you looked at me, the wall was what you got."

The sentence hit with more force than apology had any right to. Because it was not an absolution-seeking apology. It was a statement of damage, finally named.

Hermione kept her back to him. "Yes," she said, and hated how unsteady it sounded. "So am I."

She heard nothing after that for several seconds. No footsteps. No movement. When she turned despite herself, he was no longer standing. He had slid down from the chair to the edge of the wall behind it, forearms braced on his knees, head bowed as if the body had simply run out of instructions.

No apology followed. None would have been enough. The room had already taken everything it could take.

Hermione walked out.

Crossing from marble to flagstone felt like stepping out of a cellar and into ordinary weather. The corridor stayed fixed around her: portraits, stone, cracked flagstone, guest wing ahead. Nothing shifted. The house had no need. It had already shown her what it wanted shown.

At the crack in the floor, the resonance caught again, altered beyond recognition. For weeks it had lived at the edge of her attention as warmth. Now it came through as cold stone, held breath, and a grief dense enough to feel like weight. He was still in the drawing room.

She kept walking.
secondary_character_beats: Theo Nott as a full person: post-war Slytherin navigating suspicion, pragmatic, bitter in flashes, genuinely loyal. He sees the situation with Hermione immediately and says almost nothing about it except one honest line to Draco alone
state_deltas_required: The aftermath of trauma confrontation — both characters navigating a new register of honesty while trying to continue the work
Tenderness enters without permission. Domesticity becomes habitual. A third register emerges between them: not professional, not traumatic, but quiet and almost fond
